title: 111 and 222 GKIDS Blu Ray
wikitext:
[]: 1.11 & 2.22 GKIDS Blu-Ray             Quote Post by DTGee » Sat Sep 20, 2025 3:19 pm This was announced a while ago, but no one ever made a thread about it. After years of being out-of-print GKIDS is finally re-releasing 1.11 and 2.22 on blu-ay on October 21st. https://gkids.com/films/evangelion111/ https://gkids.com/films/evangelion222/ The Shout Factory listings say it includes "the most current English version", so it looks like the Funimation dub won't be included. All of the same bonus features from the Funimation release seem to be here though, save for the "Fly Me to the Moon" trailer on 1.11 and the dub cast commentary on 2.22.

[]: Re: 1.11 & 2.22 GKIDS Blu-Ray             Quote Post by C.A.P. » Wed Oct 22, 2025 3:41 pm Updating this thread to alert it’s all out. Might try to get ‘em as the month (and next month) goes on.

[]: Re: 1.11 & 2.22 GKIDS Blu-Ray             Quote Post by Diego the Lego » Wed Oct 29, 2025 7:07 pm I'm not sure what the licensing issues are on 3.33 are though since it seems to be the only one to not get a physical release from Gkids yet. Hopefully one day though.

[]: Re: 1.11 & 2.22 GKIDS Blu-Ray             Quote Post by TheFriskyIan » Tue Nov 11, 2025 9:36 am Diego the Lego wrote: I'm not sure what the licensing issues are on 3.33 are though since it seems to be the only one to not get a physical release from Gkids yet. Hopefully one day though.

[]: Re: 1.11 & 2.22 GKIDS Blu-Ray             Quote Post by Into the Eva-Verse » Fri Nov 14, 2025 11:28 am I recently ordered 1.11, 2.22, and 3.0+1.11 Collector's Edition from GKIDS. I also got 3.33 on eBay since they have yet to release that title. The first 2 movies are currently backordered, but once they're in stock again, the items will ship! I pray it's sooner rather than later.

[]: Re: 1.11 & 2.22 GKIDS Blu-Ray             Quote Post by weskerredfield3 » Sat Nov 15, 2025 3:34 am Just received my copies of 1.11 and 2.22 from Amazon yesterday, I Went through 1.11 briefly and in case anyone is wondering i can confirm that the Dub is the same one that is on Amazon Prime with the Original FUNi dub no where to be found. On the Menu, the setup defaults to Japanese with English Subs so if you want to watch it dubbed you would have to change it from the Main Menu or change the Audio track while watching the movie. The same would apply to 2.22 as well in terms of Audio Options; the Releases go very well with my Japanese Blu-Ray of 3.333 and the GKIDS Collectors edition of 3.0+1.11 and i am glad that the releases are now all consistent with each other. If you care about the original FUNi dubs, be sure to hold on to your old BDs as i do not foresee GKIDS reissuing the first 3 rebuilds with those dubs anytime soon

[]: Re: 1.11 & 2.22 GKIDS Blu-Ray             Quote Post by Diego the Lego » Sat Nov 15, 2025 9:17 pm Thanks for the info @weskerredfield3

[]: Re: 1.11 & 2.22 GKIDS Blu-Ray             Quote Post by DTGee » Tue Nov 25, 2025 1:13 am TheFriskyIan wrote: It's been a while so I may have forgotten, did the rights with FUNimation expire? Usually those are contracts that take a while to change hands (hence why 1.11 & 2.22 went so long without a rerelease).

[]: Re: 1.11 & 2.22 GKIDS Blu-Ray             Quote Post by GentlemanX » Tue Nov 25, 2025 10:33 am OOP doesn't mean the license lapsed nor do home video/television rights equate to streaming rights. While we don't know for sure how long FUNimation's rights were for, the deal with Amazon was likely uninvolved with GKids' eventual home video license. If it was, they would have released all three films closer to the initial release of 3.0+1.11. It's more likely the films were licensed after FUNimation's license expired and the specifics of the release were approved by khara (this part could have held things up too). 3.0 is likely taking longer because FUNimation's license hasn't expired yet/expired later and the release is still going through approvals. The home video release of 3.0 wasn't until 2016, so a ten year license expiring in 2026 is a distinct possibility even if we can't confirm it.

[]: Re: 1.11 & 2.22 GKIDS Blu-Ray             Quote Post by DTGee » Wed Dec 03, 2025 4:16 pm 3.33 wasn't out of print though. The Funimation/Crunchyroll stores still had copies for sale up until a year or two ago, and yet they still wiped all mention of it outside the store the moment Amazon got the rights. It's possible Funimation could've still had the rights, but I don't buy it.
